126240.explanation: For SAM, quality is more important than a numbers game. We want a strong and diverse council that collaborates and makes decisions in the interest of Waadhoeke. Whether that is exactly 50/50 men-women is not a goal in itself for us.

126241.explanation: A referendum can be a good tool to involve residents in important choices. Not everywhere and always, but used carefully. Residents should be able to have influence on decisions that affect their village or neighborhood.

126244.explanation: We believe it is important that products grown or produced by our farmers are made available as easily and locally as possible! The crux lies in the word ‘always.’ It is simply not always possible, for example, to open a campsite, especially if neighbors live very close by.

126245.explanation: Generating sustainable energy together fits Waadhoeke. We support local initiatives because they create support, lower costs, and local control.

126246.explanation: We are not in favor of establishing large distribution centers in the open Waadhoeke landscape.

126256.explanation: Farmland is intended for agriculture or livestock farming. This is also regulated in zoning plans. Of course, it is possible to place a (pre-) care home on one's own land.

126259.explanation: Good learning happens in good school buildings. That is why in recent years we have allocated much funding for new schools in Franeker (AMS, Simon Vestdijk, Kabas), Menaam, and Winsum. Many schools have also been improved. This remains a priority because it is necessary for children to grow up well in Waadhoeke!

126260.explanation: We believe that all children and youths should be able to participate in sports and culture without barriers and for free. This helps them develop talent, meet others, and be happier and more resilient. With free Aktyf Waadhoeke, we have already shown this is possible!

126261.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable. When difficulties arise, we look together at what is needed. Not automatically money, but support that fits the situation. Free Aktyf Waadhoeke also gives clubs a nice boost!

126264.explanation: Safety in villages and neighborhoods is important. Therefore, we first look at how supervisors (BOA’s), police, youth workers, and residents can cooperate better. If expansion is necessary, we do not exclude that, but more supervisors is not a goal in itself.

126265.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the temporary camera surveillance currently present during events is sufficient.

126270.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. Permanent residence does not belong there and causes uncertainty and extra pressure on facilities. The solution for the housing shortage, in our view, lies in building affordable and suitable homes, not in converting recreational properties into residences.

126271.explanation: We see that there are still plenty of possibilities for housing construction within existing village and city boundaries. This allows us to keep space available for agriculture and nature.

126272.explanation: We indeed expect social commitment from people who are mentally and physically able to contribute value to society. This can also be a good springboard to paid work. We do keep an eye on people who cannot do this. They must continue to receive this safety net.

126273.explanation: We believe we should help those who have more difficulty keeping up (socially and financially). So we also think that people without financial means should have their taxes waived.

126276.explanation: We see that there are too few charging points for electric cars in the municipality. We really need to catch up here.

126277.explanation: In other places, we see that a maximum speed of 30 km/h greatly contributes to traffic safety. Knowing that in Friesland there have been 30 traffic deaths in recent years and speed is identified by experts as a cause, we have a concrete point of attention.

126278.explanation: For several reasons, we do not support this statement. Not every person is equal, but all are of equal value! Therefore, origin or belief should not matter. Besides, this choice is practically impossible due to the tight labor market.

126282.explanation: Waadhoeke has one of the lowest tourist taxes in the Netherlands. But qualitatively, we may be one of the more interesting municipalities with no less than 2 world heritage sites, a fantastic wide-open landscape, and good hospitality and shops.

126285.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit have just as much right to social rental housing as other groups designated as urgent house hunters according to the Frisian urgency regulation. Nothing more, but certainly nothing less.

126287.explanation: Banning goes too far for us. But SAM does believe this should be the last option. We want to first look at possibilities for solar panels on, for example, parking lots, park & ride locations, road embankments, and other unused spaces.

126289.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to start a business, we are not fundamentally against establishing a coffeeshop. It is currently grown and traded in shady, undermining networks. Drugs are part of today's reality. Therefore, we choose openness, knowledge, and care, not fear and punishment.

126290.explanation: SAM is fully committed to social housing. We see a great shortage here, and especially our youth and other people with lower incomes are the victims.

126291.explanation: What is a high residential building? In one place, four floors may already be too high; in another place, 24 floors may be appropriate. We believe building should fit the environment. In the Waadhoeke context, high residential buildings are not appropriate.

126292.explanation: For years we have suffered from slumlords renting rooms to labor migrants. This results in too few homes available for Waadhoeke residents seeking housing. Therefore, in recent years, we have adjusted the policy on labor migrant housing and ended room rentals.

126294.explanation: We would like visitors to park in the center for a short visit, but if they stay longer, they should, for example, park at the AH, De Trije, or the town hall. This is walkable for most people, leaving sufficient parking capacity available.

126296.explanation: Residents near the airbase must be able to live without serious nuisance. Unfortunately, after the arrival of the F35, the nuisance has increased enormously again. This can partly be solved by properly insulating the houses. We expect the national government to pay the costs. Defense is after all a national responsibility.

126240.explanation: Suitability, quality, and cooperation are decisive for us in composing the council. Diversity is important and self-evident, but we do not opt for fixed quotas. Officials must be chosen based on trust, experience, and support.

126241.explanation: For major decisions affecting many residents, we believe people should be able to make their voices heard directly. A referendum can be a valuable addition to early participation, provided it is well prepared, clearly defined, and respects the role of the municipal council.

126244.explanation: Entrepreneurship in rural areas deserves space, but not without limits. We opt for customization per location, with attention to the environment, livability, and support. ‘Always allowing’ does not fit careful spatial policy.

126245.explanation: We believe in smart sustainability together. Local and joint energy initiatives, for example on business parks or in villages, deserve active support because they are affordable, feasible, and locally supported.

126246.explanation: Waadhoeke needs space for economic development and employment. We do not block business activity in advance but assess new initiatives on impact, integration, and added value for the local economy.

126256.explanation: Sharing plots and living on one's own grounds can contribute to livable villages and housing flow. This fits within our policy, provided it is carefully integrated and appropriate to the environment and landscape.

126259.explanation: Good, modern, and sustainable school buildings are a basic facility. We want to continue investing in this so that education is future-proof and children receive good education close to home.

126260.explanation: Culture and music contribute to development and equal opportunities. We believe it is important that every child can come into contact with this, regardless of background or income.

126261.explanation: Sports clubs are the backbone of our community. Besides practical support, financial aid may be necessary to keep clubs future-proof.

126264.explanation: Visible enforcement directly contributes to the feeling of safety. More supervisors ensure better livability, traffic safety, and approachability in villages and neighborhoods.

126265.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a goal in itself. We primarily choose visible presence, good lighting, and cooperation with residents to increase safety.

126270.explanation: Allowing permanent residence undermines both housing and tourism policy. We choose to add real homes instead of shifting uses.

126271.explanation: Infill where possible and expand where necessary. Sometimes building outside the core is needed to meet housing demand and keep villages livable. We want to be able to offer plots in every core.

126272.explanation: We choose support and perspective instead of mandatory counter-performance. Helping people towards work or participation requires trust and guidance.

126273.explanation: Basic livelihood security comes first. Residents with a low income must not be put under additional pressure due to the abolition of remission schemes.

126276.explanation: Electric driving requires sufficient charging infrastructure. We want to expand this smartly, suited to the environment and without nuisance to livability.

126277.explanation: Traffic safety requires tailored solutions per street and village. A generic 30 km/h rule does not do justice to local differences and situations.

126278.explanation: Quality and accessibility of care come first. Tailored solutions are desirable where possible, but care must remain equal and professional for everyone.

126282.explanation: Tourism should generate benefits for Waadhoeke, not be restricted. First, ensure better spending and cooperation before increasing burdens.

126285.explanation: We focus on additional housing construction instead of pitting groups against each other. Equal opportunities and clarity are the starting points.

126287.explanation: We protect fertile agricultural land and give priority to rooftops and infrastructure. At the same time, we do not fully exclude tailored solutions.

126289.explanation: This topic is not an explicit priority in our program. Any choices require careful consideration and regional coordination.

126290.explanation: We want everyone in Waadhoeke to find suitable and affordable housing. This requires tailored solutions per village and settlement, not fixed percentages. By building what is really needed - social rental, middle rental, and affordable owner-occupied housing - we ensure flow and keep villages livable.

126291.explanation: High-rise buildings may be appropriate in some places but are not a goal in themselves. We consider scale, environment, and livability per location.

126292.explanation: Excessive subdividing of homes into multiple rooms harms housing quality and livability. We want to set clear limits here to keep neighborhoods and villages livable.

126294.explanation: Accessibility and livability must be balanced. Sufficient parking space is needed for a lively and accessible center.

126296.explanation: Residents must not suffer from noise nuisance. We focus on soundproofing, with co-financing from the national and provincial governments.

126240.explanation: The quality of a candidate alderman is paramount for FNP. For too long, the mistaken belief existed that men would be better suited than women. That is nonsense. Although quality always comes first, FNP would like to see the number of female and male aldermen balanced.

126241.explanation: Residents must be involved and consulted early on subjects that concern them. Subsequently, the municipal council, as elected representatives, makes its own consideration in the general interest of the entire community. FNP is not in favor of referenda.

126244.explanation: Although FNP Waadhoeke supports giving farmers space to start other activities at their businesses (such as a shop or a campsite), this position is too black-and-white. Each situation must be assessed based on the activity and whether it is reasonably possible in that context.

126245.explanation: FNP wants to encourage all residents to save energy and also to generate sustainable energy. Especially people with low incomes should be able to participate in initiatives to make their homes more sustainable. The municipality should generously support and guide them in this.

126246.explanation: Large distribution and data centers (rectangular boxes) spoil the landscape in many parts of the Netherlands. FNP believes that such enterprises, which hardly create employment, should not be granted (building) permits in our municipality.

126256.explanation: The number of farms losing their agricultural function is growing. FNP does not want to build houses on agricultural land but does want to provide space on farmyards and in vacated farm buildings, for example for apartments, recreation, or a limited number of homes.

126259.explanation: The municipality has included all school buildings in a schedule. This schedule indicates when investment is needed in each building. If special circumstances require it, departure from the schedule should be possible and funds granted earlier.

126260.explanation: Waadhoeke’s villages and neighborhoods have a great culture of brass bands. FNP Waadhoeke values it greatly when children get the chance to experience making (music together). For this reason, FNP supports subsidies for Seewyn Arts Education and Gratis Aktyf Waadhoeke.

126261.explanation: The statement is black-and-white. Sports clubs should primarily be self-sufficient. That does not mean support is out of the question. The municipality should cooperate with sports clubs and may also provide (incidental) financial assistance, e.g., for making facilities more sustainable.

126264.explanation: Villages and neighborhoods must be safe. Where safety is threatened, enforcement officers and/or police act. FNP Waadhoeke wants the municipality to take the initiative to lobby in The Hague with other Frisian municipalities for a broader police presence in rural areas.

126265.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a goal in itself and should only be deployed where it is indispensable for safety.

126271.explanation: FNP prefers infill development for housing construction. Sometimes infill is not an option. Then villages must also have the chance to build for their youth, families, and elderly. The time when only large centers were allowed to expand is, in FNP’s view, over.

126272.explanation: The statement is black-and-white. FNP wants people not to be forced, but helped and encouraged. When people don’t have to constantly fight to make ends meet, mental space arises to look further and orient more broadly (for example, towards work or volunteer work).

126273.explanation: In FNP’s view, economic setbacks should not cause poorer health, exclusion from society, or loss of social bonds. Therefore, municipal taxes should be waivable so that people have more budget to participate.

126276.explanation: Whether we like it or not, we must make sustainability improvements to keep our world livable. Electric driving is part of this. Therefore, more facilities must be created to charge cars. Also, policies should be developed for charging cars at home (for example, with cable ducts).

126277.explanation: In many villages in Waadhoeke, residents complain about speeding and unsafe situations. At the same time, quite a few people find 30 kilometers too slow. FNP chooses safety over speed and therefore wants a maximum speed of 30 km/h in built-up areas.

126278.explanation: Although care should be appropriate, there is insufficient (financial) capacity for everyone to always choose what suits them. Efficiency in home care must improve. It must be prevented that numerous different home care organizations drive around unnecessarily to provide care.

126282.explanation: Tourist tax in Waadhoeke was introduced recently. For now, we see no reason to increase this tax.

126285.explanation: Priority is intended for people who urgently need housing, such as those fleeing domestic violence, coming from care, or homeless. It concerns a small portion of social rental housing and helps achieve good distribution in neighborhoods. Refugees are only one of these priority groups.

126287.explanation: FNP chooses clean energy with residents and respect for the landscape. Projects only succeed if villages and cooperatives participate and benefit. The municipality is greening business parks with greenery and energy hubs, researching the Climate Landscape, and seeking alternatives to solar fields.

126290.explanation: We want people to be able to remain living in their own neighborhood. Therefore, we prioritize residents with local ties in rental and also in owner-occupied new construction. For new construction, we choose 30% social rent and affordable starter homes, with mixed neighborhoods of rental and owner-occupied homes.

126291.explanation: Within the municipality of Waadhoeke, residential flats hardly fit. For FNP, five residential floors is the maximum in Franeker.

126292.explanation: FNP took the lead in ending room-by-room rental (for short stay) to labor migrants in villages and neighborhoods. We continue to closely monitor that the new rules are strictly enforced and that entrepreneurs can develop short-stay housing on their business parks.

126296.explanation: The Air Base is responsible for the noise nuisance. Defense must provide financial resources enabling residents in the area to soundproof their homes against noise. Where this proceeds sluggishly, the municipality should assist residents in achieving this nonetheless.

126240.explanation: The composition of the executive board should be based on quality, experience, and suitability, with equal opportunities for men and women.

126241.explanation: The CDA prefers to engage in dialogue and seeks cooperation.

126244.explanation: Farmers should be given space for ancillary activities, but always through customized solutions. It must fit the landscape, village, and surroundings, with attention to community support.

126245.explanation: Energy cooperatives or village initiatives deserve municipal support for decentralized energy generation. This promotes sustainability, increases support, and ensures profits remain within the community. We do not support large-scale Climate Landscapes.

126246.explanation: The CDA chooses a strong local economy. New businesses can provide employment and must fit the landscape, infrastructure, and livability.

126256.explanation: The CDA does not want cluttering of the landscape. A house at a farm on the farmer's own premises should be possible.

126259.explanation: Good, safe, and sustainable school buildings are an investment in equal opportunities and the future of our children.

126260.explanation: Music lessons contribute to broad development. The CDA believes every child should have contact with this. The offer should align with local music associations.

126261.explanation: Sports clubs are important. The municipality must support clubs that are struggling in an equal and fair manner, with clear rules for everyone.

126264.explanation: The current staffing is sufficient. More enforcement does not always mean more safety.

126265.explanation: Camera surveillance can help with safety but must be used carefully, respecting privacy.

126270.explanation: Permanent residence/living in holiday homes puts pressure on tourism and living conditions and is not a structural solution.

126271.explanation: Small-scale expansion, such as adding 'a street', should remain possible if it fits the village and landscape.

126272.explanation: Socially meaningful work helps people participate in society and feel valuable, with attention to their personal situation.

126273.explanation: The CDA stands for livelihood security. People with low incomes should not be additionally burdened.

126276.explanation: Residents who cannot charge their electric car on their own property must be able to request a public charging station, provided parking pressure does not increase.

126277.explanation: Traffic safety is important. Customized solutions per street and situation remain necessary.

126278.explanation: People must be able to choose care that fits their faith or beliefs. That is the human dimension.

126282.explanation: The CDA believes that the proceeds from tourist tax should flow back to tourism and recreation.

126285.explanation: It is a legal obligation. Moreover, it concerns a very small group.

126287.explanation: The CDA does not want solar panels or solar parks on (fertile) agricultural land. This is also why we oppose the climate landscape near Sexbierum.

126289.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit the scale and character of Waadhoeke.

126290.explanation: More social rental housing is needed, but we must build according to demand.

126291.explanation: More apartment buildings can help solve the housing shortage, especially for starters and the elderly. This must be done carefully, with good integration.

126292.explanation: Subdivision of homes into multiple units can cause nuisance and pressure on neighborhoods. Waadhoeke already has policies on this.

126294.explanation: More parking in the center increases congestion. The CDA opts for good parking facilities just outside the center, within a 5-minute walking distance from shops and amenities.

126296.explanation: Sound insulation around the airbase is the responsibility of Defence, not the municipality.

126240.explanation: The new board of mayor and aldermen must consist of quality administrators. The gender ratio is less important here.

126241.explanation: A referendum for residents is not necessary if the council and municipal government listen well to their residents. Additionally, referendum questions are one-sided, lack context, and turnout is low.

126244.explanation: Agricultural entrepreneurs must be able to operate. If they want to start a farm campsite or shop, the municipality should set as few requirements and rules as possible and enable a farm to open a shop or campsite.

126245.explanation: VVD Waadhoeke supports initiatives by residents to generate sustainable energy locally. Financial support can already be received through national subsidies. Therefore, a municipal subsidy is not needed.

126246.explanation: If a company wants to establish itself in the municipality, that should be possible, also for distribution centers. It will be assessed whether this fits within a business park or redevelopment. This creates employment and strengthens the regional economy. Provincial or national rules are followed.

126256.explanation: If a farmer wants to build on their own land, for example a second farm house, that should be possible. To solve the housing shortage, building must also take place in rural areas, including on agricultural land.

126259.explanation: Good education starts with good housing. This is recorded in a multi-year program. Additionally, current plans by the council majority for relocating AMS and De Kabas in Franeker are too ambitious.

126260.explanation: Primary schools determine their own educational content. Increasingly more schools already pay attention to music and culture, for example through Stichting Seewyn. VVD Waadhoeke therefore does not consider extra subsidies necessary.

126261.explanation: (Sports) clubs are very important for the local community. VVD Waadhoeke therefore favors supporting (sports) clubs in need, provided the cause is clear and a well-founded plan exists.

126264.explanation: More enforcement officers (boa's) were hired last council term. Undermining and vandalism continue to increase. VVD Waadhoeke wants more resources for boa's but believes their efforts should mainly focus on safety, for example around the AZC in Sint Annaparochie.

126265.explanation: Initiatives from entrepreneurs' or other associations to install cameras in business parks, shopping areas, or public spaces must be seriously investigated by the municipality.

126270.explanation: A residency obligation already applies to regular homes. For holiday homes, a permit must be requested annually as a tailored solution, and living is only allowed as a temporary measure.

126271.explanation: Building must also take place in rural areas to solve the housing shortage. Building only within village or city boundaries causes green space to disappear and reduces livability. Therefore, small streets or neighborhoods should be considered at the edges of villages or cities.

126272.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits can also contribute to society through socially useful or volunteer work. They must be obliged to reintegrate through programs at social services.

126273.explanation: VVD Waadhoeke prefers to help people with low incomes through (budget) coaching to overcome financial problems. This requires a tailored approach, allowing for occasional waiving of municipal taxes. This is more valuable in the long term than automatic waivers.

126276.explanation: In the coming period, there will be increasingly more electric cars. The municipality has therefore already established charging points within the municipality, which is sufficient. Furthermore, charging an electric car remains a private initiative.

126277.explanation: VVD Waadhoeke is against lowering the maximum speed limit within built-up areas. The same applies to downgrading roads in rural areas.

126278.explanation: No, general assistance. VVD Waadhoeke is against even more additional rules or requirements for healthcare professionals.

126282.explanation: The tourist tax will not increase further. One euro per person per night is sufficient. This is linked to the registration requirement for overnight stays.

126285.explanation: Young people and starters have difficulty finding housing, also in the social rental sector. VVD Waadhoeke supports, within legal frameworks, allocating homes first to local youth before to status holders.

126287.explanation: Agricultural land should be used for farming, not for solar panels. These are better placed on the roofs of businesses or homes. Solar parks also burden the energy grid. The provincial regulation is followed.

126289.explanation: No, that is already centralized in Leeuwarden.

126290.explanation: VVD Waadhoeke adheres to the national guideline of 30%. There should be a mix of social rental and owner-occupied homes built in the municipality.

126291.explanation: VVD Waadhoeke's starting point is that only what is desirable and fits the surroundings is built, in terms of nature and scale. This is done in consultation with residents, whose input is genuinely listened to.

126292.explanation: It must remain possible to rent rooms for student housing. However, renting rooms to labor migrants must be prohibited. VVD Waadhoeke wants labor migrants to receive housing arranged by their employer.

126294.explanation: Due to the increase of tourists and visitors to Franeker's center, more parking spaces should, where necessary, be provided, with attention also for tour buses. More parking spaces must be created during the redesign of the area around the train station.

126296.explanation: The municipality must work with the Ministry of Defence to secure funding for soundproofing houses, as well as facilities in Marsum and around the Leeuwarden airbase. The costs are a national responsibility, not a municipal one.

126240.explanation: Expertise is an important quality; an equal distribution between men and women benefits the overall quality.

126241.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. Moreover, advisory referendums are not binding, which causes confusion and dissatisfaction. A (broad) survey is a better alternative.

126244.explanation: The municipality supports the exercise of all enterprises, including other activities on a farmyard, considering the circumstances in terms of nature and scale.

126245.explanation: We provide opportunities for citizen initiatives and support local energy communities.

126246.explanation: If such a case arises, it must be assessed by nature and scale in consultation with residents and other entrepreneurs.

126256.explanation: Large-scale construction outside built-up areas is only allowed after a careful procedure. A single home should in principle be possible within reasonable limits.

126259.explanation: Many of these buildings require (regular) maintenance. This should not be postponed.

126260.explanation: All children should be able to play sports or participate in culture together as much as possible and be able to attend school. This provides hope and stability. This does not mean the municipality must pay for every child.

126261.explanation: If other options, such as collaborating with other associations, have been explored, then a financial contribution under conditions is negotiable but not automatic.

126264.explanation: This can be an appropriate solution to improve the feeling of safety in certain locations.

126265.explanation: No additional camera surveillance should be installed; it is a possible form of supervision.

126270.explanation: This is not a structural solution because it detracts from the recreational offer. Temporarily, it can be a solution for acute emergencies.

126271.explanation: To meet the high demand for housing, building outside built-up areas should be possible, but always through a careful process.

126272.explanation: People receiving benefits who, for example, do volunteer work, should not be penalized by withholding benefits. However, not everyone has the opportunity to do something socially meaningful.

126273.explanation: That could make the financial problems of these people possibly worse than they already are, with all the consequences.

126276.explanation: Especially in neighborhoods where charging on private property is not possible.

126277.explanation: This increases the safety of all road users.

126278.explanation: This makes care better aligned with the experiences of the residents.

126282.explanation: Not as a means to generate revenue. Only if it contributes to the public interest around (residential) recreation.

126285.explanation: Rules concerning urgent housing allocation apply to all residents. However, as Waadhoeke, we want to adhere to national agreements regarding the housing of status holders.

126287.explanation: Other locations must first be investigated and utilized. For example, combining wind and solar energy.

126289.explanation: The ChristenUnie opposes the presence of coffee shops and/or the cultivation of cannabis by municipalities.

126290.explanation: However, the percentage must at least remain equal to what it is now. So if homes disappear from the supply, others must replace them.

126291.explanation: That can be a solution to the housing problem. However, the environment’s design must be taken into account. Sufficient greenery and enough play space.

126292.explanation: Renting out individual rooms can be a good option. However, the rights and obligations of all parties involved must be considered.

126294.explanation: In consultation with the residents of the center, a suitable form must be found.

126296.explanation: This is a Defence responsibility. The municipality can do everything possible to keep this issue on Defence’s radar and monitor that it is addressed.

126240.explanation: BBB believes the council should primarily consist of capable people who can govern reliably and with integrity, regardless of gender.

126241.explanation: BBB has an open attitude towards referendums; however, Waadhoeke already has a good platform where residents can make their voices heard: stemvanwaadhoeke.nl. BBB wants to use this platform more often.

126244.explanation: BBB gives farmers the opportunity to realize new sources of income. The plans must, of course, be feasible and in harmony with the surroundings.

126245.explanation: Local cooperatives and initiatives are an important link in the transition to more sustainable energy. The municipality must support this, for example through subsidies, free permits, or tax exemptions.

126246.explanation: Although BBB puts the local entrepreneur first, these kinds of businesses provide a large amount of employment for the region. Naturally, BBB only agrees when the plans are appropriate and in agreement with the neighbors.

126256.explanation: BBB wants to make it possible and easier for farmers to build an (additional) house on their own property.

126259.explanation: School buildings that no longer meet the standards must be improved by the municipality.

126261.explanation: BBB wants to support these sports clubs, but a joint effort towards a healthy financial situation is necessary.

126264.explanation: BBB advocates a strict approach to public nuisance offenders, with BOAs (Special Enforcement Officers) being essential in collaboration with the Police.

126265.explanation: BBB wants more camera surveillance in places where there is a safety risk. BBB opposes camera surveillance throughout the entire public space.

126270.explanation: Utilizing holiday homes for permanent residence is a quick and easy step to increase the housing supply.

126271.explanation: BBB focuses as much as possible on infill development. Where that is not possible, adding a street is the right solution, even if it shifts the village boundary. The land for this must be acquired voluntarily; BBB opposes expropriation.

126272.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits must contribute to society wherever possible.

126273.explanation: BBB does not want to burden people with low incomes with even higher costs.

126276.explanation: BBB wants to continue the current strategy in cooperation with the province.

126277.explanation: BBB wants an appropriate maximum speed. Signals from the neighborhoods are leading in whether or not to lower the maximum speed.

126278.explanation: People should have as much freedom as possible in choosing care. However, the costs must remain within reasonable limits.

126282.explanation: BBB does not want to increase the tourist tax. Prices for an overnight stay have already risen enormously; the municipality should not let this increase further.

126285.explanation: BBB gives priority to its own residents.

126287.explanation: BBB does not allow agricultural land to be lost to solar parks.

126289.explanation: Although coffeeshops can contribute to reducing illegal trade, BBB (taking into account national policy advice) sees no room for a coffeeshop in the municipality. In areas where retail is allowed, a coffeeshop would be too close to a school or childcare facility.

126290.explanation: BBB does not believe in such a strict norm. Supply and demand must be balanced, with a suitable place for everyone. More social rental homes than currently available are needed for this. This requires strong agreements with housing corporations and developers.

126291.explanation: BBB opposes tall residential buildings; they do not fit the character of our city and villages. Slightly taller apartments may be possible, but only where they fit and do not cause nuisance.

126292.explanation: BBB in principle allows this, but only where it is responsible. Sacrificing family homes in neighborhoods for rooms is not responsible; dividing larger buildings into rooms can be.

126294.explanation: According to BBB, Franeker has sufficient parking availability. Combined, there is enough parking in the inner city and the areas just outside it (Town Hall, Leeuwarderweg, and Hertog v. Saxenlaan).

126296.explanation: BBB believes the Ministry of Defence is responsible here as the cause.

statements.title: The municipality should spend money on soundproofing houses around Leeuwarden Air Base.
The residents of Waadhoeke should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Waadhoeke moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Waadhoeke.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
At least 50% of newly built homes in Waadhoeke should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Waadhoeke moet minimaal 50% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Waadhoeke should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the center of Franeker.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Waadhoeke.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.
